response.justification: The candidate is a seasoned polyglot engineer with a genuine track record in developer tooling — including real IDE work on NI LabWindows/CVI and self-built language tooling (test runners, assertion libraries) — but the core stack requirement is not met. His depth is in TypeScript/Node.js and DLang, with only incidental Java exposure and no evidence of Kotlin, Spring, or deep JVM expertise, which are hard requirements for this enterprise Java/Kotlin IDE role.
strengths: Direct IDE development experience (C/C++ components for NI LabWindows/CVI)
Demonstrated passion for language tooling and testing infrastructure (Trial test runner, Fluent-Asserts, TDD advocacy)
Long, varied track record across complex systems, from low-latency microservices to full products
concerns: No demonstrated Kotlin or Spring ecosystem experience — both required qualifications
Java appears only peripherally in his history; no evidence of deep JVM internals knowledge
Recent work centers on web/AI-agent tooling in TypeScript, far from the enterprise JVM domain
